message.text:
With story 7.1, the UX isn't quite there yet. We have a giant list of referenced by up top, then the current code below without syntax highlighting for known languages.

In this tool I'm less interested in seeing what the code looks like and more interested in seeing how the code has been relevant for various things and seeing what refers to what in almost a graph type of view.

For repositories that are hosted somewhere, I'd love to be able to link to a source file online, but this is going to need another story defined in epics and sprint-status (inside this epic) and rely on some configuration - though we could do things like detecting when we're on GitHub pages and link automatically to the appropriate file in GitHub.

2. The raw source code below (line-numbered, but NO syntax highlighting)

## Story 7.1 (In-Portal Code Browsing) — How the Source File Page Is Built

### 1. The page renderer — `CodeFileTemplater.cs`

`C:\Dev\SpecScribe\src\SpecScribe\CodeFileTemplater.cs` renders the Source File page. It is a synthesized page (no markdown backing) that builds its own HTML shell rather than going through `HtmlTemplater.RenderPage`.

Key members:
- **`RenderPage(repoRelativePath, outputRelativePath, lines, nav, referencedBy)`** — lines 22-49. Renders the full page:
  - Line 33: the line-count pill — `<div class="meta-pills"><span class="pill">{count} line(s)</span></div>`.
  - Line 36: calls `AppendReferencedBy`.
  - Lines 38-46: the raw code block. Emits `<pre class="code-file"><code>` then, per source line, a `<span class="code-line" id="L{n}">` wrapping a `<span class="code-ln">{n}</span>` (the line number) and `<span class="code-src">{HTML-escaped source}</span>`. **No syntax highlighting / no tokenization** — the code comment (lines 12-14) explicitly states "syntax-readable" means monospace + line numbers + preserved whitespace, not colored tokens. Every line gets a GitHub-compatible `id="L{n}"` anchor (1-based) for deep-linking (`#L42`).
- **`AppendReferencedBy(sb, prefix, referencedBy)`** — lines 55-69. Emits `<aside class="code-referenced-by">` with an `<h2>Referenced by</h2>` and a `<ul>` of `<li><a href=...>title</a></li>`. An empty/null list writes nothing (no bare heading). Note the on-page heading text is "Referenced by" (the ALL-CAPS "REFERENCED BY" you see is CSS text-transform styling).
- **`RenderPlaceholder(...)`** — lines 75-89. For binary/oversized/unreadable files; keeps the full shell but replaces the code table with an explanatory `<p class="code-placeholder">`.
- **`BeginShell(...)`** — lines 94-115. Emits head + nav bar + breadcrumb (`Home` / repo-path), opens `<main id="main-content">` and `<header class="doc-header">`, and writes the page title: line 112 `<div class="story-kicker">Source File</div>` and line 113 `<h1>{repoRelativePath}</h1>`.

Styling lives in `C:\Dev\SpecScribe\src\SpecScribe\assets\specscribe.css` — `pre.code-file` (line 473), `.code-line` / `.code-line:target` (493-503, the `#L{n}` highlight), `.code-ln` (505), `.code-src` (515), `.code-referenced-by` (531-556).

### 2. How "Referenced by" relationships are computed

The referenced-by data is a citation-driven reverse map, computed in `C:\Dev\SpecScribe\src\SpecScribe\SiteGenerator.cs`.

Data model fields (lines 54-67):
- `_codePages` (`Dictionary<string,string>`) — forward map: repo-relative source path → code-page output path (`code/<path>.html`).
- `_codeReferenced` (`List<string>`) — the deterministic, sorted set of referenced files that get a page.
- `_codeReverseMap` (`Dictionary<string, List<(string CitingSourceRelative, string Title)>>`) — the file → citing-artifacts back-map that drives each page's "Referenced by" block. Keyed on the citing artifact's source-relative path.

**`DiscoverCodeReferences(sourceFiles)`** — lines 711-773. Runs UP FRONT (invoked at line 172, before any page renders). For each `*.md` source artifact it:
- Reads the raw markdown, computes the citing artifact's source-relative path and its display title via `ExtractArtifactTitle` (lines 781-785 — first Markdown H1, else the path).
- Calls `CodeReferenceScanner.ExtractTargets` to pull `[Source: …]` citations, resolves each via `CodeReferenceScanner.TryResolveCitation`, adds surviving paths to `referenced` and appends `(citingRelative, citingTitle)` to `_codeReverseMap[repoRel]` (deduped per artifact).
- Populates `_codePages[repoRel] = code/<repoRel>.html` (lines 766-772).

**`BuildReferencedBy(repoRelative)`** — lines 885-901. At render time, resolves each citing artifact's source-relative path to its output URL by reusing the epics render pass's `_referenceMap` (line 1125; populated at line 1013), falling back to `PathUtil.ToOutputRelative`. Returns the `(OutputUrl, Title)` list passed to `CodeFileTemplater.RenderPage`.

### 3. The citation scanner — `CodeReferenceScanner.cs`

`C:\Dev\SpecScribe\src\SpecScribe\CodeReferenceScanner.cs` is pure/disk-free citation discovery (Story 7.1, FR15):
- Records: `CodeCitation(Target, RelativeToArtifact)` (line 10), `CitedArtifact(RawMarkdown, ArtifactDirFullPath)` (line 14).
- Regexes: `SourceMarkdownLink` (lines 26-28, `[Source: [x](../../src/X.cs)]`), `SourceInline` (lines 33-35, `[Source: \`src/X.cs:15-17\`]`), `LineSuffix` (line 39, strips `:N`/`:N-M`).
- **`ExtractTargets(markdown)`** — lines 45-59.
- **`StripLocator(target, out int? line)`** — lines 70-92, strips `#fragment` then `:line`, reporting the first line for `#L{n}` deep-linking.
- **`Discover(...)`** — lines 100-121, and **`TryResolveCitation`** (130-141), **`TryResolveRepoFile`** (148-155), **`TryResolvePath`** (161-183). The gate: candidate must (a) resolve inside `RepoRoot`, (b) NOT be under `SourceRoot` (`_bmad-output` docs render as their own pages), (c) exist as a file, (d) not be an ignored working file (`PathUtil.IsIgnoredSourceFile`, `PathUtil.cs` lines 29-36 — dotfiles, `~$`, `.tmp`, `.crswap`).

Important: the whole repo is deliberately NOT walked. Only files explicitly cited via `[Source: …]` get a page (NFR1).

### 4. How code files are read and turned into pages

**`GenerateCodePagesInternal(sourceFiles, nav, reporter)`** — `SiteGenerator.cs` lines 796-877, invoked at line 206.
- Wipes/recreates the `code/` output dir (atomic rebuild).
- Iterates `_codeReferenced`; output path is `code/<repoRelative>.html` (extension included, so `X.cs`/`X.ts` don't collide).
- Traversal defense-in-depth check (829-837).
- Reads file: `>MaxCodeFileBytes` (line 698: `1_048_576` ≈ 1 MB) → placeholder; else `TryReadCodeText` (lines 906-938 — binary detection via NUL byte or strict-UTF-8 decode failure, BOM stripped); on success `SplitCodeLines` (lines 943-952 — normalizes `\r\n`/`\r` to `\n`, drops a single trailing empty line) then `CodeFileTemplater.RenderPage`.
- Per-file try/catch emits `GenerationEvent`s; on failure the `_codePages` entry is removed so no citation links to a 404.

### 5. Cross-reference / "what refers to what" graph data

Yes — this already exists, computed up front:
- **Story → file (and any-artifact → file):** `_codeReverseMap` in `SiteGenerator.cs` (line 67) — file → list of citing artifacts (source-relative path + title). This IS the referenced-by graph. Its inverse (artifact → files) is implicit in `DiscoverCodeReferences`' per-artifact citation loop.
- **Forward file → page map:** `_codePages` (line 58).
- **Story 7.2 linkifier** — `C:\Dev\SpecScribe\src\SpecScribe\CodeReferenceLinkifier.cs` — resolves inert `[Source: …]` citations and view-source `<a>` links across every generated page into `code/<path>.html#L{n}` (or external `{CodeSourceBaseUrl}/<path>#L{n}`). Invoked from **`ApplyReferenceLinks`** (`SiteGenerator.cs` lines 1808-1825), the same whole-page post-process that runs `RequirementLinkifier` and `StoryEpicLinkifier`.

Note there is no file→file citation graph — citations only originate from `*.md` artifacts, so the graph is artifact→file (and the reverse file→artifact). A separate change-coupling graph (file co-change) exists under the deep-git analytics feature (`DeepAnalyticsTemplater`), but that is unrelated to Story 7.1's citation graph.

### How the pieces connect (pipeline order)
1. `DiscoverCodeReferences` (line 172) — builds `_codePages` + `_codeReverseMap` before any page renders (so citations resolve everywhere).
2. Epics/story/doc/ADR/etc. pages render, each passed through `ApplyReferenceLinks` → `CodeReferenceLinkifier` (turns citations into links to the code pages).
3. `GenerateCodePagesInternal` (line 206) — renders the actual `code/<path>.html` pages via `CodeFileTemplater`, each with its `BuildReferencedBy` block.

### Where syntax highlighting would be added
The single insertion point is `CodeFileTemplater.RenderPage`, lines 38-46 — the loop currently emits `PathUtil.Html(lines[i])` inside `<span class="code-src">` with zero tokenization. Any highlighting (server-side tokenizer, or a client JS highlighter + `<script>`/CSS) would hook in there. The comment at lines 12-14 documents the deliberate current absence of highlighting and client JS.</result>

# BMad Epic 7 Structure — Findings for Adding a New Story 7.x

## 1. The epics document

**`_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md`** is the single canonical epics file (a large ~1700+ line doc holding all epics inline). There is no per-epic split file. A copy also exists inside a git worktree at `.claude/worktrees/bmad-create-story-7-1-caac2f/_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md` — ignore that; the repo-root one is authoritative.

### Epic 7 definition (epics.md line 1217)

- **Title:** `## Epic 7: Code and Git Exploration` (note: "Exploration" is misspelled "Exploration"→"Exploration" — actually spelled **"Exploration"** in the file... it literally reads **"Exploration"**; the file's exact text is "Code and Git Exploration" — see below)
- **Exact heading text (line 1217):** `Epic 7: Code and Git Exploration`
- **Goal (line 1219):** "Let users browse the project's code and history in-portal — turning source citations into navigable code pages and dates into activity timelines, with advanced code-and-git coverage as an opt-in depth — so the portal explains not just what is planned, but what exists and what happened when."
- **FRs covered (line 1221):** FR14, FR15, FR16, FR19

FR context (epics.md lines 176–181): FR14 = source-code treemap; **FR15 = in-portal code file browsing and source-citation linking to code pages**; FR16 = activity timeline/per-date pages; FR19 = advanced code-and-git coverage.

### All Story 7.x stories (from epics.md + sprint-status.yaml)

| Story | Title | Status (sprint-status.yaml) | Story file |
|-------|-------|------|------|
| 7.1 | In-Portal Code File Browsing | **review** | `7-1-in-portal-code-file-browsing.md` |
| 7.2 | Source-Citation and Comment Linking to Code Pages | **review** | `7-2-source-citation-and-comment-linking-to-code-pages.md` |
| 7.3 | Activity Timeline and Date Pages | **in-progress** | `7-3-activity-timeline-and-date-pages.md` |
| 7.4 | Advanced Code and Git Coverage | **ready-for-dev** | `7-4-advanced-code-and-git-coverage.md` |
| 7.5 | Per-Commit Detail Pages | **ready-for-dev** | `7-5-per-commit-detail-pages.md` |
| 7.6 | Source Code Treemap for Codebase Exploration | **ready-for-dev** (moved from Story 3.4 per SCP 2026-07-08) | `7-6-source-code-treemap-for-codebase-exploration.md` |

Epic 7 itself is `epic-7: in-progress`; `epic-7-retrospective: optional`. The epics.md Epic 7 section (lines 1217–1365) contains the user-story + acceptance-criteria text for all six stories (7.1 AC at 1235-1247, 7.2 AC at 1260-1272, etc.).

## 2. Story 7.1 and 7.2 (the code-link strategy owners)

Both story files are in `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/`. The memory note is confirmed exactly by the files:

**Story 7.1 (`7-1-in-portal-code-file-browsing.md`, Status: review)** owns **the setting + the generation gate**:
- Introduces `CodeSourceBaseUrl` (nullable, non-`required`) on `ForgeOptions`, CLI option `--code-url <BASE>` on `SiteSettings`, and persistence via `SettingsStore`/`SavedSettings` (`CodeUrl`), CLI-wins-over-saved (7.1 lines 51-66, 83, 156-158, 249-252).
- **Strategy:** `CodeSourceBaseUrl` unset (default) → generate in-portal `code/<repo-relative-path>.html` pages; set → **skip** in-portal generation entirely (external mode) (7.1 lines 53-57).
- Locks the shared **`#L{n}` line-anchor convention** (`id="L{n}"`, 1-based) — identical in both in-portal and external modes (7.1 lines 60, 74, 267, 271).
- Built `CodeReferenceScanner`, `CodeFileTemplater`, `_codePages` map (repo-relative → output-relative), `GenerationPhase.CodePages`. Deliberately does NOT rewrite citations — that's 7.2.
- Already implemented (Dev Agent Record present; 815 tests green; 82 code pages rendered in a real pass).

**Story 7.2 (`7-2-source-citation-and-comment-linking-to-code-pages.md`, Status: review)** owns **resolution**:
- New `CodeReferenceLinkifier` wired into `SiteGenerator.ApplyReferenceLinks` (whole-page pass). Resolves citations to `{prefix}code/<repo-rel>.html#L{n}` (in-portal, gated on `_codePages` membership) OR `{CodeSourceBaseUrl.TrimEnd('/')}/<repo-rel>#L{n}` (external, gated on file existing in `RepoRoot`) (7.2 lines 54-61, 228, 290).

- Added a `DiscoverCodeReferences` pass that runs BEFORE epics/doc/ADR render so `_codePages` is populated when citations linkify.
- Already implemented (837 tests green, +21).

## 3. sprint-status.yaml

**Path:** `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml`

**Structure:**
- Leading comment block (lines 1-41) documents status definitions: Epic statuses (`backlog`/`in-progress`/`done`), **Story statuses** (`backlog`, `ready-for-dev`, `in-progress`, `review`, `done`), retrospective statuses (`optional`/`done`), action-item statuses.

- **`development_status:`** map (line 50) — flat map where each epic and each story is a key. Epic key form `epic-N: <status>`; story key form `<N>-<M>-<slug>: <status>`, plus `epic-N-retrospective: <status>`. Many entries carry trailing `# …` inline comments (some extremely long — the Epic 6 entries have multi-line paragraph comments).
- **`action_items:`** list (line 248) — objects with `epic`, `action`, `owner`, `status`.

```

  7-2-source-citation-and-comment-linking-to-code-pages: review # dev-story 2026-07-12: …
  7-3-activity-timeline-and-date-pages: in-progress

  7-5-per-commit-detail-pages: ready-for-dev
  7-6-source-code-treemap-for-codebase-exploration: ready-for-dev # moved from Story 3.4 (SCP 2026-07-08)
```
Stories are listed in numeric order directly under the `epic-7:` line, followed by `epic-7-retrospective: optional`. **To add a new story you would add a `7-7-<slug>: backlog` (or `ready-for-dev`) line after `7-6-…` and before `epic-7-retrospective:`.**

## 4. Existing Epic 7 story files, location & naming convention

**Location:** all story files live flat in **`_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/`** (same dir as `sprint-status.yaml`, epic retros, and `spec-*.md` correction files). This matches `story_location` in the yaml.

**Naming convention:** `<epic>-<story>-<kebab-slug>.md` (e.g. `7-1-in-portal-code-file-browsing.md`). No zero-padding; dash separators; slug derived from the story title.

**Epic 7 files present** (all six exist):
- `7-1-in-portal-code-file-browsing.md`
- `7-2-source-citation-and-comment-linking-to-code-pages.md`
- `7-3-activity-timeline-and-date-pages.md`
- `7-4-advanced-code-and-git-coverage.md`
- `7-5-per-commit-detail-pages.md`
- `7-6-source-code-treemap-for-codebase-exploration.md`

**Story file structure** (consistent across files): YAML frontmatter with `baseline_commit:` → `# Story N.M: Title` → `Status: <status>` → `## Story` → `## Acceptance Criteria` → `## Developer Context` → `## Technical Requirements (Dev Agent Guardrails)` (DO/DON'T) → `## Architecture Compliance` → `## Library / Framework Requirements` → `## File Structure Requirements` → `## Testing Requirements` → `## Previous Story Intelligence` → `## Git Intelligence Summary` → `## Latest Technical Information` → `## Project Context Reference` → `## Tasks / Subtasks` → `## Dev Notes` → `## Dev Agent Record` → `## Change Log`. (These context-rich files are produced by the `bmad-create-story` skill / `create-story` workflow.)

## 5. Is there already a story about linking to externally-hosted source (GitHub) / repo base URL config?

**No dedicated/standalone story.** The external-GitHub-hosting capability is **not its own story** — it is the `CodeSourceBaseUrl` setting (CLI `--code-url <BASE>`), split across the two existing stories exactly per the memory note:
- **7.1 owns** the setting definition, plumbing (`ForgeOptions`/`SiteSettings`/`SettingsStore`), and the generation gate (skip in-portal pages when a base URL is set).
- **7.2 owns** the resolution: emitting `{CodeSourceBaseUrl}/<repo-rel>#L{n}` GitHub-style links in external mode.

The grep for `CodeSourceBaseUrl`/`code-url`/`github.com` matched only: `sprint-status.yaml`, the 7.1/7.2 story files, `7-4-…`, `7-6-…` (which reference the code-page/link infra), and `4-8-generation-diagnostics-and-configuration-log-page.md` (diagnostics logging of the setting). Explicitly **out of scope** in both stories: auto-deriving the base URL from `git remote get-url origin` (7.1 lines 65, 88; 7.2 line 66) — flagged as a possible future enhancement, so a *new* Story 7.x for git-remote auto-detection of the repo base URL would be a natural, currently-unclaimed addition.

## How to add a new Story 7.x (summary)

1. **epics.md** — add a `### Story 7.7: <Title>` block under Epic 7 (after the Story 7.6 block, before `## Epic 8:`), following the `As a … / I want … / So that …` + numbered **Acceptance Criteria** (Given/When/Then/And) pattern used by 7.1-7.6.
2. **sprint-status.yaml** — add `7-7-<slug>: backlog` (or `ready-for-dev`) after the `7-6-…` line, before `epic-7-retrospective:`.
3. **Story file** — create `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/7-7-<slug>.md` with the frontmatter + section structure shown above (normally via the `bmad-create-story` / `create-story` workflow).
4. **Consistency note:** Epic 6 Action Item (sprint-status.yaml lines 297-304, status `open`) explicitly warns that structural scope changes must be written to **both** epics.md and sprint-status.yaml in the same change — a renumber/add recorded in only one artifact is treated as a drift bug. Also note the append-only / no-renumber convention used throughout (vacant slots like Story 3.4 and 5.4 are left intentionally vacant rather than reused).

## SpecScribe — Configuration System & Code-Citation Linking

Important framing note: the **main working tree is already on `main` and already contains full implementations of both Story 7.1 (setting + gate) and Story 7.2 (resolution)**. The `--code-url` / external-GitHub-base-URL feature you're describing is **already built**, not greenfield. There is a separate in-progress worktree at `.claude/worktrees/bmad-create-story-7-1-caac2f/` (detached HEAD `a4df7c8`) — an older/parallel copy; all paths below are the authoritative `src/SpecScribe/` main tree.

### 1. Configuration model & system

Config is a layered flow: **CLI options → resolved options → generator**, with optional JSON persistence and a `config.toml` read for branding.

- **`src/SpecScribe/SiteSettings.cs`** — the CLI-facing options class (`SiteSettings : CommandSettings`, Spectre.Console.Cli). Properties: `Source`, `Adrs`, `Output`, `ProjectName`, `NoReadme`, `DeepGit`, `Spa`, and `CodeUrl` (`--code-url`, lines 37-39). `Resolve()` (line 43) forwards everything into `ForgeOptions.Resolve(...)`.

- **`src/SpecScribe/ForgeOptions.cs`** — the resolved, absolute-path settings model consumed by rendering (`sealed class ForgeOptions`). Key members: `RepoRoot`, `SourceRoot`, `AdrSourceRoot`, `OutputRoot`, `SiteTitle`, `IncludeReadme`, `DeepGitAnalytics`, `EmitSpa`, and `CodeSourceBaseUrl` (line 49).
  - **TOML loading lives here**, not a parser library: `ReadProjectName(string repoRoot)` (lines 209-224) reads `_bmad/config.toml` and extracts `project_name` via a single compiled regex `ProjectNamePattern` (lines 203-205). Constants `ConfigDirName = "_bmad"` (line 67) and `ConfigFileName = "config.toml"` (line 68). Comment at 207-208 explicitly notes "A full TOML parser would be overkill for one key." So the only config.toml value the tool reads is `project_name` → `SiteTitle`.
  - `Resolve(...)` (lines 98-150) does repo-root discovery (walks up from cwd looking for `_bmad-output`), ADR-directory probing, and sets `CodeSourceBaseUrl = codeSourceBaseUrl is { Length: > 0 } ? ... : null` (line 148).

- **`src/SpecScribe/SettingsStore.cs`** — optional persistence to a `.specscribe` JSON file (`FileName = ".specscribe"`). `SavedSettings` (lines 8-27) mirrors the user-choosable options including **`CodeUrl` (line 22)**. `TryLoad()`, `TrySave(SiteSettings)`, and `ApplyTo(saved, settings)` (line 95) — with CLI options always winning over persisted values (`settings.CodeUrl ??= saved.CodeUrl`, line 105).

- **`src/SpecScribe/FileWatcherService.cs`** — the config.toml watcher. `WatchedExtensions` includes `.toml` (line 15). It watches `_bmad/` for `config.toml` specifically when that dir exists (lines 40-44), in addition to the `_bmad-output` source root and `docs/adrs`. Debounced at `ForgeOptions.DebounceInterval` (400 ms).

- **Wiring into rendering — `src/SpecScribe/Commands.cs`**: `settings.Resolve()` → `new SiteGenerator(options)` → generate (lines 15/25, 55/60, 256). Interactive path: `RunMenu` (line 300) calls `SettingsStore.TryLoad()` + `ApplyTo` (lines 306-309), and `ConfigurePaths` (lines 361-385) persists via `TrySave`.
  - **Parity gap worth flagging for planning:** the interactive "Configure paths" menu (`ConfigurePaths`, lines 361-385) prompts for Source, Adrs, Output, ProjectName, and DeepGit — but **NOT `CodeUrl`**. `CodeUrl` is reachable only via the `--code-url` CLI flag (and then persisted). `CodeUrl`/`code-url` appears in **no** interactive file (`ConsoleUi.cs`, `Program.cs`, `Commands.cs`, `BmadCommands.cs` all have zero matches). The `--deep-git` menu prompt (lines 377-378) cites "NFR7 (menu/CLI parity)"; by that same standard the external-URL setting is currently CLI-only.

### 2. External repository base URL setting / gate / resolution — ALREADY EXISTS

The "7.1 owns setting+gate, 7.2 owns resolution" split is implemented:

- **Setting (7.1):** `--code-url <BASE>` → `SiteSettings.CodeUrl` → `ForgeOptions.CodeSourceBaseUrl`. Doc string in `SiteSettings.cs:38`: *"Base URL for source-file links (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo/blob/main). When set, in-portal code pages are NOT generated and citations link to {BASE}/<path>#L<line> instead."*
- **Gate (7.1):** `src/SpecScribe/SiteGenerator.cs`
  - `DiscoverCodeReferences` (line 711) — early-returns when `_options.CodeSourceBaseUrl is { Length: > 0 }` (line 719), leaving `_codePages` empty.
  - `GenerateCodePagesInternal` (line 796) is skipped in external mode (per its doc at 794-795).
- **Resolution (7.2):** `src/SpecScribe/CodeReferenceLinkifier.cs` — `Linkify(...)` (line 55). `external = codeSourceBaseUrl is { Length: > 0 }` (line 65); `baseUrl = codeSourceBaseUrl!.TrimEnd('/')` (line 69). `TryBuildHref` (lines 136-160) builds `baseUrl + "/" + repoRel + fragment` in external mode (line 150) vs. `prefix + outputRelative + fragment` in in-portal mode (line 158).
- **Shared `#L{n}` anchor:** generated identically in both modes — `fragment = line is { } n ? "#L" + n : ""` (`CodeReferenceLinkifier.cs:141`). The first line number is parsed from a `:N`/`:N-M` locator by `CodeReferenceScanner.StripLocator(target, out int? line)` (`CodeReferenceScanner.cs:70-92`). In-portal code pages emit matching `id="L{n}"` anchors (see `CodeFileTemplater.cs`).

### 3. Code-citation link generation & the resolution seam

- **Discovery/parse:** `src/SpecScribe/CodeReferenceScanner.cs` — pure, disk-free extraction of `[Source: …]` citations (`ExtractTargets`, line 45) in two shapes: markdown-link (`SourceMarkdownLink`, line 26, artifact-relative) and inline/code-span (`SourceInline`, line 33, repo-relative). `Discover` (line 100) and `TryResolveCitation` (line 130) apply the inside-repo / not-under-`_bmad-output` / exists / not-ignored gate. `TryResolveRepoFile` (line 148) is the external-mode existence check.
- **The link-resolution seam** — `src/SpecScribe/SiteGenerator.cs`, method **`ApplyReferenceLinks(...)` (line 1808)**. This whole-page post-process runs on every generated page (30+ call sites, e.g. lines 592, 680, 1054, 1485). It runs the FR/Story linkifiers first, then calls `CodeReferenceLinkifier.Linkify(html, _codePages, _options.CodeSourceBaseUrl, prefix, _options.RepoRoot, _options.SourceRoot)` (lines 1822-1823). `_codePages` (field at line 58) is the forward map populated by `DiscoverCodeReferences`.
- **In-portal page rendering:** `src/SpecScribe/CodeFileTemplater.cs` renders `code/<repo-rel>.html` line-numbered pages with per-line `id="L{n}"` anchors (Story 7.1, FR15).
- **Parallel doc-citation resolver (not code):** `src/SpecScribe/SourceLinkifier.cs` handles `.md` doc-citation links and is deliberately kept separate from code-file linking (noted in `CodeReferenceLinkifier.cs:22-25`).

### 4. Deployment-target / GitHub Pages awareness — NOT in application code

- There is **no in-code detection** of GitHub Pages, `github.io`, base-href, or site root. Grep for `github.io|baseHref|<base|gh-pages` across `src/` returns only doc-comment mentions (e.g. `ForgeOptions.cs:45`, `72`) — no logic.
- The only Pages knowledge is the CI workflow **`.github/workflows/publish-docs-live-pages.yml`** — builds with `--deep-git`, uploads `SpecScribeOutput`, deploys via `actions/deploy-pages@v5`. It does **not** pass `--code-url`, so the deployed site currently uses in-portal code pages.
- SPA output uses **root-relative** URLs, not an injected base href: `src/SpecScribe/assets/specscribe-spa.js:18` ("resolves against the site root"). No `<base>` tag injection exists anywhere.

### 5. Git remote / origin detection — DOES NOT EXIST

This is the key gap for auto-detecting a GitHub repo:

- Git is invoked only through **`RunGit(workingDirectory, arguments)`** in `src/SpecScribe/GitMetrics.cs:567` (`ProcessStartInfo { FileName = "git" }`, line 571-573).
- All four call sites read history only — no remote/config:
  - `rev-list --count HEAD` (line 115)
  - `log --pretty=format:... --date=...` (line 126)
  - `log --name-only --pretty=format: -n 200` (line 139)
  - `log ...` for deep analytics (line 276)
- There is **no** `git config --get remote.origin.url`, `git remote get-url`, or any read of the remote/origin anywhere in `src/`. So the tool has no existing mechanism to auto-derive a GitHub base URL from the git remote — a planned auto-detection feature would be net-new, most naturally added alongside `GitMetrics.RunGit` (the existing, timeout-guarded, failure-tolerant git-process seam) and fed into `ForgeOptions.CodeSourceBaseUrl` as a fallback when `--code-url` is unset.

### Summary for planning

The external-GitHub-base-URL feature (`--code-url`, `CodeSourceBaseUrl`, gate in `SiteGenerator.DiscoverCodeReferences`/`GenerateCodePagesInternal`, resolution in `CodeReferenceLinkifier`, shared `#L{n}` anchor) is **fully implemented** on `main`. What does **not** exist and would be the likely subject of new planning: (a) **git-remote auto-detection** of the base URL (no code reads `remote.origin.url`), (b) **GitHub Pages / base-href deployment awareness** in-app, and (c) **interactive-menu parity** for the `CodeUrl` setting (currently CLI-only, unlike every other configurable option).</result>

Here is Claude's plan:

# Story 7.1 Rework + New Story 7.7: Relationship-First Code Pages & External Source Linking

## Context

Story 7.1 (In-Portal Code File Browsing) shipped a code page (`code/<repo-rel>.html`) that
leads with a long **Referenced by** list and then dumps the raw, un-highlighted source. The
owner's intent for this tool is inverted from that emphasis: the code page should foreground
**how a file relates to the rest of the project** ("what refers to what," as a graph), not be a
code viewer. Separately, for repos hosted on a platform (GitHub/GitLab), the page should link
out to the real source file online — with the base URL auto-detected rather than hand-configured.

Two important findings from exploration shape this plan:

1. **The external-URL setting already exists but as a *replacement*, not an add-on.** Stories 7.1/7.2
   (both in `review`) implemented `--code-url` → `ForgeOptions.CodeSourceBaseUrl`. When set, it
   *skips* in-portal code pages entirely and rewrites every citation straight to GitHub. The owner
   wants **additive** behavior — keep in-portal browsing *and* add a "View on GitHub" link — which
   is a deliberate change to that shipped-but-not-final gate.
2. **There is no git-remote or GitHub-Pages awareness anywhere in the code.** Auto-detection is
   genuinely net-new. Git is only ever invoked through `GitMetrics.RunGit` (history reads only).

Owner decisions (this session): additive "View on GitHub"; the relationship view is a **visual
node-link SVG graph**; base URL auto-detected from **git remote + GitHub Pages/CI context** (explicit
`--code-url` always wins); split as **reopen Story 7.1** (UX/graph redesign) **+ new Story 7.7**
(external linking + detection).

---

## Part A — Reopen Story 7.1: relationship-first code page

Redesign the code page so relationships are the hero and raw source is secondary. Owner of the
page template: **`src/SpecScribe/CodeFileTemplater.cs`** (`RenderPage`, `AppendReferencedBy`).

**Layout change (`RenderPage`, lines 30–48):**
- **Elevate** the relationships block to directly under the header as the primary content.
- Replace the flat `<ul>` "Referenced by" (`AppendReferencedBy`, lines 55–69) with a **pure-SVG
  node-link graph**: this file as the center node, one surrounding node per citing artifact, each
  node a real link to that artifact's page. This honors the project's charting rule — **pure SVG +
  links, no JS** (memory: `charting-is-pure-svg-no-js`) — and routes any color through the existing
  status/ink tokens (memory: `specscribe-status-token-system`). Keep the semantic `<ul>` of links
  as an accessible fallback beneath/within the graph (screen-reader parity, NFR6/UX-DR16 — links
  must keep meaningful text, never "click here").
- **De-emphasize** the raw code: move the `<pre class="code-file">` below the graph and make it
  visually secondary (e.g. a collapsible "View source" region). **Hard constraint:** the per-line
  `id="L{n}"` anchors (lines 42–44) and the `code/<path>.html` URL **must be preserved** — Story 7.2
  deep-links to `#L{n}` and that anchor scheme is a locked cross-story convention. If the source is
  collapsed, ensure `#L{n}` navigation still lands (default the region open, or rely on the
  browser's scroll-into-closed-`<details>` behavior — verify in the preview).

**Graph data — already computed, no new pipeline:**
- The `(OutputUrl, Title)` list passed as `referencedBy` comes from `SiteGenerator.BuildReferencedBy`
  / `_codeReverseMap`, populated up front by `DiscoverCodeReferences` (`SiteGenerator.cs` 740–802).
- **Scope reality to state honestly (memory: `create-story-elicit-visual-intent`):** citations are
  **artifact→file only** — there is *no* file→file citation edge. So the graph is "this file ←
  stories/epics/ADRs/docs that cite it." Do not imply a call/dependency graph.
- *Optional enrichment:* to group/color nodes by artifact **type** (story vs epic vs requirement vs
  doc), derive type from the citing artifact's output path/title (cheap, deterministic) rather than
  adding new scan data. Decide during dev; the neutral single-type graph is acceptable for v1.

**Preserve the VS Code re-targeting seam:** the epics.md comment on 7.1 (lines 1225–1229) requires
code links to carry `data-code-path` / `data-line` so a host can re-target them. Keep that seam
intact in the redesigned markup.

**Styling:** extend the existing `.code-referenced-by` / `pre.code-file` rules in
`src/SpecScribe/assets/specscribe.css` (~lines 473–556); add the graph's SVG/node styling there.

## Part B — New Story 7.7: additive external source linking + auto-detection

**B1. Flip the gate from replacement to additive.** Today `CodeSourceBaseUrl` set ⇒ no in-portal
pages and citations point external. Change so in-portal pages are **always** generated and the base
URL is *additive*:
- `SiteGenerator.DiscoverCodeReferences` (lines 748–751): remove the external-mode early return so
  the page map is always built.
- `SiteGenerator.GenerateCodePagesInternal` (824+): always render pages.
- `CodeReferenceLinkifier.Linkify` / `TryBuildHref` (`CodeReferenceLinkifier.cs` 55–160): citations
  resolve to the **in-portal** page (which itself links out); external base no longer diverts them.
- **Add a "View source on GitHub ↗" link** on the code page, slotting into Part A's redesigned
  header, pointing to `{baseUrl}/{repoRel}` (shared `#L{n}` anchor scheme). Rendered only when an
  effective base URL exists. (This is 7.7's addition into 7.1's template — sequence 7.1 first.)
- **Impact to flag in the story:** this supersedes the replacement semantics of 7.1/7.2 and will
  break existing tests that assert "external mode skips pages / citations go external." Those tests
  are rewritten for additive behavior. (Both stories are still `review`, so this is in-bounds.)

**B2. Auto-detect the base URL** (precedence: explicit `--code-url` > CI/Pages env > git remote > none):
- **Git remote:** add one `git remote get-url origin` (or `git config --get remote.origin.url`) call
  via the existing timeout-guarded `GitMetrics.RunGit` seam (`GitMetrics.cs` 574+). Parse both HTTPS
  (`https://github.com/owner/repo(.git)`) and SSH (`git@github.com:owner/repo.git`) forms for GitHub
  and GitLab → `https://<host>/owner/repo/blob/<branch>`. Get `<branch>` from
  `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` with a `main` fallback. Failure-tolerant (like ADR probing) —
  any failure ⇒ no auto URL, never throws.
- **GitHub Pages / CI context:** detect via env vars present in the Pages workflow (`GITHUB_ACTIONS`,
  `GITHUB_SERVER_URL`, `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, `GITHUB_REF_NAME`) to build the blob base even when the
  local remote differs. (The workflow `.github/workflows/publish-docs-live-pages.yml` does not pass
  `--code-url` today; detection makes the deployed site link out automatically.)
- **Wiring:** a small, pure, well-tested helper (e.g. `CodeSourceUrlResolver.TryDetect(repoRoot)`)
  invoked from `ForgeOptions.Resolve` (`ForgeOptions.cs` 98–150) only when `codeSourceBaseUrl` is
  null. Keep remote parsing separated from the git process call so it's unit-testable on strings.

**B3. Menu/CLI parity (NFR7).** `CodeUrl` is currently CLI-only. Add it to the interactive
`ConfigurePaths` in `src/SpecScribe/Commands.cs` (~361–385), matching the `--deep-git` prompt
pattern, and surface the auto-detected/effective URL so the user can confirm or override.

**B4. Diagnostics.** Update the generation/config log (Story 4.8 page) to report the detected source
and the effective base URL (explicit vs detected vs none).

---

## Story & artifact bookkeeping

Structural changes must land in **both** `epics.md` and `sprint-status.yaml` in the same change
(Epic 6 action item treats single-artifact drift as a bug). Append-only numbering — do not reuse slots.

- **`_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md`**
  - Reopen 7.1: extend its Acceptance Criteria (lines 1235–1247) with the relationship-first /
    graph / de-emphasized-source ACs.
  - Add `### Story 7.7: External Source Linking and Auto-Detection` after the Story 7.6 block
    (before `## Epic 8:`, line 1367), following the As-a / I-want / So-that + Given/When/Then AC
    pattern used by 7.1–7.6.
- **`_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml`** (Epic 7 block, lines 137–144)
  - `7-1-in-portal-code-file-browsing: review` → `in-progress` (reopened).
  - Add `7-7-external-source-linking-and-auto-detection: ready-for-dev` after the `7-6-…` line,
    before `epic-7-retrospective:`.
  - (This file is not strictly-valid YAML — the `{project-root}` lines — so edit it as text.)
- **Story files** in `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/` (naming `<epic>-<story>-<slug>.md`):
  - Update `7-1-in-portal-code-file-browsing.md` (Status → `in-progress`, new ACs, Change Log entry).
  - Create `7-7-external-source-linking-and-auto-detection.md` with the full context-rich section
    structure the other Epic 7 files use.
  - **Author these via the `bmad-create-story` skill** (it produces the standard structure and works
    in a git worktree). Note: this repo's `main` has a background auto-committer that pushes edits
    straight to `main`, so prefer the worktree flow for story authoring (memory:
    `worktree-edits-must-target-worktree-path`).

## Primary files to modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/SpecScribe/CodeFileTemplater.cs` | 7.1: graph-first layout, de-emphasized source, keep `#L{n}` anchors + `data-code-path`/`data-line` seam; 7.7: "View on GitHub" link slot |
| `src/SpecScribe/assets/specscribe.css` | 7.1: node-link graph + secondary-source styling (~473–556) |
| `src/SpecScribe/SiteGenerator.cs` | 7.7: remove external-mode gates in `DiscoverCodeReferences` (748–751) & `GenerateCodePagesInternal` |
| `src/SpecScribe/CodeReferenceLinkifier.cs` | 7.7: citations resolve in-portal in additive mode (55–160) |
| `src/SpecScribe/CodeSourceUrlResolver.cs` *(new)* | 7.7: remote/CI parse → blob base URL (pure, unit-tested) |
| `src/SpecScribe/GitMetrics.cs` | 7.7: add `remote get-url origin` / branch read via `RunGit` (574+) |
| `src/SpecScribe/ForgeOptions.cs` | 7.7: call detection when `codeSourceBaseUrl` null; update `CodeSourceBaseUrl` doc (42–49) |
| `src/SpecScribe/Commands.cs` | 7.7: `CodeUrl` in interactive `ConfigurePaths` (~361–385) |

## Verification

1. **Build & unit tests:** `dotnet test`. Add/adjust tests for: remote parsing (GitHub+GitLab,
   HTTPS+SSH+`.git` suffix), env/CI precedence, additive gate (pages generated *even when* a base URL
   is set), the "View on GitHub" href shape, and the graph markup (nodes = citing artifacts, anchors
   preserved). **Regenerate the committed `GoldenContentFingerprint`** — the code-page HTML changes,
   which trips the byte-parity golden test (memory: `golden-diff-normalization-gotchas`).
2. **Real generation:** run the CLI to regenerate `SpecScribeOutput` (default output dir — memory:
   `generate-output-dir-is-specscribeoutput`), then open `code/src/SpecScribe/Charts.cs.html` in the
   Browser pane and confirm: (a) the relationship graph leads and each node links to the right
   artifact; (b) raw source is secondary; (c) `…Charts.cs.html#L42` still scrolls to line 42;
   (d) the "View on GitHub ↗" link resolves to the correct blob URL.
3. **Detection matrix:** verify explicit `--code-url` overrides; unset falls back to git remote; a CI
   env context (simulate the Pages env vars) produces the blob base; a repo with no remote degrades
   to no external link (in-portal only), no errors.
4. **Menu parity:** run the interactive menu, confirm `CodeUrl` is configurable and the detected
   value is shown.

## Open considerations

- **Additive removes the old external-only mode.** Owner chose additive over "configurable both
  ways," so full-replacement is intentionally dropped. If preserving an external-only site later
  matters, a `--code-url-mode` flag could re-add it — out of scope now.
- **Collapsed-source + `#L{n}`:** confirm deep-link behavior into a de-emphasized/collapsed source
  region across browsers during preview; if fragile, keep source visible-but-secondary rather than
  collapsed.
- **Graph is artifact→file only** — set node/edge labeling to reflect that truthfully; don't imply a
  code dependency graph.

# Story 7.1 Rework + New Story 7.7: Relationship-First Code Pages & External Source Linking

## Context

Story 7.1 (In-Portal Code File Browsing) shipped a code page (`code/<repo-rel>.html`) that
leads with a long **Referenced by** list and then dumps the raw, un-highlighted source. The
owner's intent for this tool is inverted from that emphasis: the code page should foreground
**how a file relates to the rest of the project** ("what refers to what," as a graph), not be a
code viewer. Separately, for repos hosted on a platform (GitHub/GitLab), the page should link
out to the real source file online — with the base URL auto-detected rather than hand-configured.

Two important findings from exploration shape this plan:

1. **The external-URL setting already exists but as a *replacement*, not an add-on.** Stories 7.1/7.2
   (both in `review`) implemented `--code-url` → `ForgeOptions.CodeSourceBaseUrl`. When set, it
   *skips* in-portal code pages entirely and rewrites every citation straight to GitHub. The owner
   wants **additive** behavior — keep in-portal browsing *and* add a "View on GitHub" link — which
   is a deliberate change to that shipped-but-not-final gate.
2. **There is no git-remote or GitHub-Pages awareness anywhere in the code.** Auto-detection is
   genuinely net-new. Git is only ever invoked through `GitMetrics.RunGit` (history reads only).

Owner decisions (this session): additive "View on GitHub"; the relationship view is a **visual
node-link SVG graph**; base URL auto-detected from **git remote + GitHub Pages/CI context** (explicit
`--code-url` always wins); split as **reopen Story 7.1** (UX/graph redesign) **+ new Story 7.7**
(external linking + detection).

---

## Part A — Reopen Story 7.1: relationship-first code page

Redesign the code page so relationships are the hero and raw source is secondary. Owner of the
page template: **`src/SpecScribe/CodeFileTemplater.cs`** (`RenderPage`, `AppendReferencedBy`).

**Layout change (`RenderPage`, lines 30–48):**
- **Elevate** the relationships block to directly under the header as the primary content.
- Replace the flat `<ul>` "Referenced by" (`AppendReferencedBy`, lines 55–69) with a **pure-SVG
  node-link graph**: this file as the center node, one surrounding node per citing artifact, each
  node a real link to that artifact's page. This honors the project's charting rule — **pure SVG +
  links, no JS** (memory: `charting-is-pure-svg-no-js`) — and routes any color through the existing
  status/ink tokens (memory: `specscribe-status-token-system`). Keep the semantic `<ul>` of links
  as an accessible fallback beneath/within the graph (screen-reader parity, NFR6/UX-DR16 — links
  must keep meaningful text, never "click here").
- **De-emphasize** the raw code: move the `<pre class="code-file">` below the graph and make it
  visually secondary (e.g. a collapsible "View source" region). **Hard constraint:** the per-line
  `id="L{n}"` anchors (lines 42–44) and the `code/<path>.html` URL **must be preserved** — Story 7.2
  deep-links to `#L{n}` and that anchor scheme is a locked cross-story convention. If the source is
  collapsed, ensure `#L{n}` navigation still lands (default the region open, or rely on the
  browser's scroll-into-closed-`<details>` behavior — verify in the preview).

**Graph data — already computed, no new pipeline:**
- The `(OutputUrl, Title)` list passed as `referencedBy` comes from `SiteGenerator.BuildReferencedBy`
  / `_codeReverseMap`, populated up front by `DiscoverCodeReferences` (`SiteGenerator.cs` 740–802).
- **Scope reality to state honestly (memory: `create-story-elicit-visual-intent`):** citations are
  **artifact→file only** — there is *no* file→file citation edge. So the graph is "this file ←
  stories/epics/ADRs/docs that cite it." Do not imply a call/dependency graph.
- *Optional enrichment:* to group/color nodes by artifact **type** (story vs epic vs requirement vs
  doc), derive type from the citing artifact's output path/title (cheap, deterministic) rather than
  adding new scan data. Decide during dev; the neutral single-type graph is acceptable for v1.

**Preserve the VS Code re-targeting seam:** the epics.md comment on 7.1 (lines 1225–1229) requires
code links to carry `data-code-path` / `data-line` so a host can re-target them. Keep that seam
intact in the redesigned markup.

**Styling:** extend the existing `.code-referenced-by` / `pre.code-file` rules in
`src/SpecScribe/assets/specscribe.css` (~lines 473–556); add the graph's SVG/node styling there.

## Part B — New Story 7.7: additive external source linking + auto-detection

**B1. Flip the gate from replacement to additive.** Today `CodeSourceBaseUrl` set ⇒ no in-portal
pages and citations point external. Change so in-portal pages are **always** generated and the base
URL is *additive*:
- `SiteGenerator.DiscoverCodeReferences` (lines 748–751): remove the external-mode early return so
  the page map is always built.
- `SiteGenerator.GenerateCodePagesInternal` (824+): always render pages.
- `CodeReferenceLinkifier.Linkify` / `TryBuildHref` (`CodeReferenceLinkifier.cs` 55–160): citations
  resolve to the **in-portal** page (which itself links out); external base no longer diverts them.
- **Add a "View source on GitHub ↗" link** on the code page, slotting into Part A's redesigned
  header, pointing to `{baseUrl}/{repoRel}` (shared `#L{n}` anchor scheme). Rendered only when an
  effective base URL exists. (This is 7.7's addition into 7.1's template — sequence 7.1 first.)
- **Impact to flag in the story:** this supersedes the replacement semantics of 7.1/7.2 and will
  break existing tests that assert "external mode skips pages / citations go external." Those tests
  are rewritten for additive behavior. (Both stories are still `review`, so this is in-bounds.)

**B2. Auto-detect the base URL** (precedence: explicit `--code-url` > CI/Pages env > git remote > none):
- **Git remote:** add one `git remote get-url origin` (or `git config --get remote.origin.url`) call
  via the existing timeout-guarded `GitMetrics.RunGit` seam (`GitMetrics.cs` 574+). Parse both HTTPS
  (`https://github.com/owner/repo(.git)`) and SSH (`git@github.com:owner/repo.git`) forms for GitHub
  and GitLab → `https://<host>/owner/repo/blob/<branch>`. Get `<branch>` from
  `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` with a `main` fallback. Failure-tolerant (like ADR probing) —
  any failure ⇒ no auto URL, never throws.
- **GitHub Pages / CI context:** detect via env vars present in the Pages workflow (`GITHUB_ACTIONS`,
  `GITHUB_SERVER_URL`, `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, `GITHUB_REF_NAME`) to build the blob base even when the
  local remote differs. (The workflow `.github/workflows/publish-docs-live-pages.yml` does not pass
  `--code-url` today; detection makes the deployed site link out automatically.)
- **Wiring:** a small, pure, well-tested helper (e.g. `CodeSourceUrlResolver.TryDetect(repoRoot)`)
  invoked from `ForgeOptions.Resolve` (`ForgeOptions.cs` 98–150) only when `codeSourceBaseUrl` is
  null. Keep remote parsing separated from the git process call so it's unit-testable on strings.

**B3. Menu/CLI parity (NFR7).** `CodeUrl` is currently CLI-only. Add it to the interactive
`ConfigurePaths` in `src/SpecScribe/Commands.cs` (~361–385), matching the `--deep-git` prompt
pattern, and surface the auto-detected/effective URL so the user can confirm or override.

**B4. Diagnostics.** Update the generation/config log (Story 4.8 page) to report the detected source
and the effective base URL (explicit vs detected vs none).

## Story 7.1 (reopened) — relationship-first code page
The code page no longer leads with a giant list + a raw code dump. It now leads with a **pure-SVG node-link graph** (`Charts.ReferenceGraph`): the file at the center, one linked node per citing artifact, with compact ring labels ("Story 7.3") and full titles on hover/in an accessible list. The raw source drops into a secondary "Source" section — kept **visible, not collapsed**, so the locked `#L{n}` deep-links still land on every browser (I verified this in-browser: the L-anchors are visible and not inside a closed `<details>`). No JS, neutral tokens only, `data-code-path` re-targeting hook preserved.

## Story 7.7 (new) — additive external linking + auto-detection
The `--code-url` behavior flipped from **replacement** to **additive**: in-portal pages always generate, citations still resolve in-portal, and each page gains a host-aware "View on GitHub ↗" link. New `CodeSourceUrlResolver` auto-detects the blob base from the **git remote** (GitHub/GitLab, HTTPS+SSH+`.git`+subgroups) or the **GitHub Pages/CI env**, with explicit `--code-url` always winning. Detection is gated so it only fires on the CLI path — the golden fingerprint stays machine-independent. Menu parity + a diagnostics row added.

- **881 tests green** (+22 new: resolver parsing/precedence, additive gate, view-source href, graph markup). Golden fingerprint rebaselined (CSS-only).

## What changed

- **No redundant list** — the visible "individual stories" list is gone; the graph (with tooltips + clickthroughs) is the surface. I kept the list as `sr-only` (1×1, off-screen) purely for screen-reader/keyboard access, since an SVG `role="img"` doesn't expose its interior links to assistive tech. `#L{n}` deep-links still land (source stays visible, never collapsed).

message.text: Could we add code colorization as well (language / extension specific?) using a JS library?

parts.text: Could we add code colorization as well (language / extension specific?) using a JS library?

response.value:
Yes, we can — but it's a more consequential change than it looks, because the code page has a few hard constraints that a drop-in highlighter fights with. Let me lay out the real decision before I build anything.

**The tensions:**
1. **Our line structure is custom.** Each line is `<span class="code-line" id="L{n}">` + a gutter span + `<span class="code-src">`. Highlighters (highlight.js/Prism) want one contiguous `<code>` block — they can't correctly color multi-line constructs (block comments, multi-line strings) when each line is highlighted in isolation. So I'll need to restructure to highlight the whole block, then re-attach the locked `id="L{n}"` anchors and the `:target` highlight that Story 7.2's `#L42` deep-links depend on. Doable, just not a one-liner.
2. **Self-contained delivery.** SpecScribe ships as a self-contained binary / npx package with assets *embedded and copied out* — no CDN. Any JS library must be bundled as an embedded resource (offline + GitHub Pages must keep working).
3. **The VS Code webview has a strict CSP** (nonce'd scripts). A client-side highlighter has to be wired through that (and the SPA adapter) or disabled there — it touches all three delivery forms.
4. It **reverses a documented decision** (the "no syntax highlighter, no client JS for code" note in `CodeFileTemplater` and memory) — fine, you're the owner, but I'll update those.

It'll degrade gracefully (no JS → today's plain monospace + anchors still work) and I'll map file extension → language (we control the path, so `.cs`→csharp etc., better than auto-detect).

The one thing I genuinely need from you is the engine, since it drives bundle size, language coverage, and CSP work:
